Those absences mean Ben Chilwell and Olivier Giroud return to the starting line-up, while Andreas Christensen gets his first chance to impress from the start under Tuchel. ‘I want them to enjoy it and not try too hard, but at the same time in their positions we expect certain reliable behaviour,’ added Tuchel. ‘For them I hope they play with a smile and they keep on going with the same attitude they have shown in training.

‘This is not a gift that we gave them, they deserve to play. More than only 11 deserve to play, this choice I have to make, and it’s always a good sign when it’s hard choices.’
